name: Red Cherry Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain cherries and reserve the juice.
2. In a saucepan, combine brown sugar and cornstarch. Stir in cherry juice, orange juice, butter, and salt until thickened.
3. Add the reserved cherry juice to the mixture and stir in the cherries and almond extract.
4. For the biscuit topping, mix together the homemade biscuit mix, sugar, and nutmeg with egg and milk until combined.
5. Heat the cherry mixture until boiling, then pour into a preheated 1-quart baking dish.
6. Drop spoonfuls of batter (about 6) onto the hot cherry mixture.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 35-40 minutes, or until the biscuits are golden brown.
8. Serve warm. Makes 6 servings.
